ucs.title: [LLM] Ajax Security Team (Rocket Kitten) trojanized anti-censorship installer dropping Stealer/CWoolger keylogger
ucs.description: Hunts Operation Saffron Rose tradecraft: a user runs a lookalike anti-censorship tool (Psiphon, Ultrasurf, Anti-Censorship Bundle, FreeGate, Proxifier) downloaded from the web or opened from an email attachment, which then spawns a child binary writing to %APPDATA% and beaconing out — Ajax's documented chain for targeting Iranian dissidents and U.S. DIB engineers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("psiphon*.exe","ultrasurf*.exe","freegate*.exe","anticensorship*.exe","proxifier*.exe","tor*.exe") OR Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Downloads\\*Psiphon*","*\\Downloads\\*Ultrasurf*","*\\Downloads\\*AntiCensorship*","*\\Temp\\*Saffron*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("psiphon*.exe","ultrasurf*.exe","freegate*.exe","anticensorship*.exe","proxifier*.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id as process_id Processes.process Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rename process as child_process process_name as child_process_name] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080) by All_Traffic.src as dest All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")` | rename dest as c2_dest] | table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name child_process_name c2_dest c2_dest as c2_ip count | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let saffronLures = dynamic(["psiphon","ultrasurf","freegate","anticensorship","anti-censorship","saffron","proxifier","plustools","stealer"]);
let suspectExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\Downloads\\","\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\")
| where FileName has_any (saffronLures) or InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (saffronLures)
| project LureTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, LureProcess=FileName, LurePath=FolderPath, LurePid=ProcessId, LureParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let keyloggerDrop = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (saffronLures)
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".dll"
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ChildProc=FileName, ChildPath=FolderPath, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, LurePid=InitiatingProcessId;
let beacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemotePort in (80,443,8080)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any ("\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\")
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, BeaconProc=InitiatingProcessFileName;
suspectExec
| join kind=inner (keyloggerDrop) on DeviceId
| join kind=inner (beacon) on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (LureTime .. LureTime + 1h) and NetTime between (LureTime .. LureTime + 6h)
| project LureTime, DeviceName, AccountName, LureProcess, ChildProc, ChildCmd, BeaconProc, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl
| sort by LureTime desc
ucs.rationale: Operation Saffron Rose (FireEye, 2014) is the defining campaign of Ajax Security Team — they specifically packaged Stealer/CWoolger inside fake Iranian anti-censorship installers (Psiphon, Anti-Censorship Bundle) to lure dissidents and U.S. DIB targets who needed to bypass Iranian censorship. The lure-set + AppData child drop + immediate beacon is a chain almost no other actor reproduces, making t

ucs.title: [LLM] Rocket Kitten browser credential harvest after phishing-via-service callback (Stealer/MPK)
ucs.description: Hunts the Ajax/Rocket Kitten post-foothold pattern where a process spawned from a phishing service link (T1566.003 — historically fake LinkedIn, Yahoo, Facebook security alerts) reads Chrome/Firefox/IE Login Data stores and stages credentials to disk for exfil — the documented behavior of the Stealer and MPK keylogger families used by this Iranian crew.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as process_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\logins.json" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\key?.db" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Vault\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\*") AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","firefox.exe","msedge.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","svchost.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Web where Web.url IN ("*linkedin-security*","*yahoo-account*","*facebook-verify*","*google-alert*","*account-recovery*") OR Web.http_user_agent="*PlusTools*" by Web.src as dest Web.url Web.http_user_agent | `drop_dm_object_name("Web")`] | table firstTime dest user process_name file_path url http_user_agent count | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let credStores = dynamic(["\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\","\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\","\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\","\\Microsoft\\Vault\\","\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\"]);
let credFiles = dynamic(["Login Data","logins.json","key3.db","key4.db","signons.sqlite","cookies.sqlite","Web Data"]);
let browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","firefox.exe","msedge.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe"]);
let credAccess = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FolderPath has_any (credStores) and FileName in (credFiles)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in (browsers)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("svchost.exe","explorer.exe","SearchProtocolHost.exe","MsMpEng.exe")
| project AccessTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ActorProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ActorPath=InitiatingProcessFolderPath, ActorPid=InitiatingProcessId, ActorCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, TargetFile=FileName;
let phishService = UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where Url has_any ("linkedin-security","yahoo-account","facebook-verify","google-alert","account-recovery","verify-mail","securemail")
| project ClickTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, ClickedUrl=Url, NetworkMessageId;
let email = EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where SenderFromDomain has_any ("linkedin","yahoo","facebook","google") and isnotempty(NetworkMessageId)
| where ThreatTypes has "Phish" or DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| project EmailTime=Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, NetworkMessageId;
credAccess
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceLogonEvents | project DeviceId, AccountName=AccountName) on DeviceId
| join kind=inner (phishService) on $left.AccountName == $right.AccountUpn
| join kind=leftouter (email) on NetworkMessageId
| where AccessTime between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + 24h)
| project ClickTime, AccessTime, DeviceName, AccountName, SenderFromAddress, Subject, ClickedUrl, ActorProc, ActorCmd, TargetFile
| sort by AccessTime desc
ucs.rationale: Ajax Security Team / Rocket Kitten / Flying Kitten is one of the few Iranian crews that historically used spearphishing-via-service (fake LinkedIn / Yahoo / Facebook 'security alert' messages — see ClearSky and Check Point reporting on Rocket Kitten 2015) followed by Stealer/MPK harvesting browser cred stores. Joining a phish-service URL click to a non-browser process opening Chrome/Firefox Login
